registry.description: Experience is the inner accumulation that comes from having actually lived through something — not just knowing about it but knowing it from the inside. The Hebrew root is primarily the word for seeing, suggesting that experience is gained through careful, attentive observation of what actually happens. The Greek adds the sense of being active, engaged. Experience as a category sits at the intersection of knowledge and memory: it is what knowledge looks like after it has been tested against reality.
registry.inference_note: Experience as a general category — the inner accumulation of perceived and felt events — is ABSENT as a biblical term. Scripture addresses specific experiences through suffering, testing, and the knowledge gained through living, but never abstracts them into a general experience category.
